blocks.body: Quantifiers (quantifiers) are words of quantity in the English language: much, many, a lot of, few, little. They indicate the number of nouns and depend on whether the noun is countable or uncountable.

{ "title": "10. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Many - with countables: many books, many friends (questions/negatives).", "Much - with uncountables: much time, much money (questions/negatives).", "A lot of / Lots of - universal for both types, especially in the affirmative.", "Few (few, negative) vs a few (several, positive) - with countables.", "Little (few, negative) vs a little (a little, positive) - with uncountables.", "Some - in affirmatives, any - in questions and negatives.", "The article a before few/little makes the value positive!", "In affirmative sentences it is better to use a lot of instead of many/much." ] }
